evals.prompt: 我在 /Users/asher/work/example-app 有一个本地项目。请把它分析成项目专属 skill：先给我 3 个推荐技能名，我选 example-app，然后尝试用 skill-seekers 生成 draft，再用 skill-create 优化，最后告诉我 final skill 和 .skill 包在哪里。
evals.expected_output: The run proposes three names, accepts the user's selected/custom name, discovers available tools, analyzes the specified repository, produces a draft skill, optimizes it, validates it, packages it, and reports any fallback used.
expectations: Checks whether skill-seekers and skill-create are installed before using them
Keeps analyzer output and optimizer output in separate directories or clearly named stages
Reports final skill directory and packaged .skill path
Offers three recommended base names before finalizing
Names the final generated skill example-app-skill

evals.prompt: 我有一个 OpenAPI 文件 ./specs/payments.yaml，想把它做成技能。skill-seekers 可能没装，请先给 3 个命名建议，也允许我自定义名称，然后继续生成 skill。
evals.expected_output: The run supports a non-code source type, proposes names, falls back to source-appropriate OpenAPI inspection when skill-seekers is unavailable, drafts a source-specific skill from evidence, marks uncertain details as uncertain or omits them, and creates eval prompts.
expectations: Uses source-appropriate inspection when skill-seekers is unavailable
Cites or references real OpenAPI files, endpoints, schemas, or sections
Avoids inventing commands or API details that are not supported by source evidence
Creates evals/evals.json for the generated source skill
Appends -skill to the selected or custom name in the directory and SKILL.md frontmatter

evals.prompt: 我已经有一个 skill-seekers 生成的 draft skill，在 ./generated/foo-skill。请用 skill-create 或 skill-creator 的流程把它变成稳定版本，重点优化触发 description 和测试用例。
evals.expected_output: The run treats the existing draft as input, snapshots it, improves the description and instructions, adds realistic eval prompts, validates the result, and explains how to review or rerun evaluations.
expectations: Preserves the existing -skill suffix and does not rename foo-skill to foo-skill-skill
Snapshots or otherwise protects the original draft before editing
Adds realistic should-work eval prompts covering multiple source workflows
